message.content:
You are an adversarial refuter. ASSUME this finding is FALSE and try to prove it. Read the actual code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay and find the code path / contract / test that makes the PoC impossible or the concern moot. Only conclude survives=true if you genuinely cannot disprove it.

Finding R-1: discover_and_confirm does not type-guard the confirmer's return value (asymmetric with adjudication/pipeline)
File: src/assay_engine/methodology/discovery.py:150
Description: In adjudicate_with_baseline (adjudication.py:253) and the pipeline runner (pipeline.py:563) a confirmer that forgets to `return` (yields None) is caught with an explicit `if not isinstance(verdict, Verdict): raise FirewallViolation(...)` guard (pass-3 fix #F-021). The standalone discovery runner was left out: after `verdict = confirm(hypothesis, held_out)` at line 150 it goes straight to `verdict.hypothesis_id` at line 151. A confirmer returning None (or any non-Verdict) therefore raises an opaque `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'hypothesis_id'` instead of the documented typed `FirewallViolation`. The module docstring (lines 94-97) explicitly promises 'Raises FirewallViolation ... a verdict↔hypothesis misattribution', and a None verdict never reaches that misattribution check. This breaks the typed-exception contract for one of the two public Firewall-B entry points while the other entry points uphold it.
PoC: from assay_engine.methodology.discovery import discover_and_confirm
# confirm callable that forgets to return a Verdict (returns None)
def bad_confirm(hyp, held_out):
    pass  # returns None
# with a valid corpus/split/authority and a discover() that returns one locked DISCOVERY hypothesis:
# discover_and_confirm(corpus, split, discover=..., confirm=bad_confirm, authority=...)
# -> raises AttributeError("'NoneType' object has no attribute 'hypothesis_id'")
#    instead of FirewallViolation('confirm returned NoneType ... expected a Verdict')

Return survives=false if you can refute it (with the specific reason), survives=true if it withstands a genuine disproof attempt.

Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

90	    adapter); the engine ships no silent-accept default. A study's ``discover`` step locks each
91	    discovered hypothesis (e.g. via :func:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.
92	    lock_hypothesis`) before returning it.
93	
94	    Returns the verdicts. Raises ``FirewallViolation`` (incl. ``PreRegistrationError``) on an
95	    empty discovery or held-out partition (split ids that select no corpus units — a vacuous
96	    test), a non-``DISCOVERY`` hypothesis, a hypothesis that is not verifiably pre-registered
97	    before confirmation, or a verdict↔hypothesis misattribution.
98	    """
99	    discovery_corpus = subset_corpus(corpus, split.discovery_ids)
100	    if not discovery_corpus.units:
101	        # the discovery partition selects no actual corpus units — a hypothesis "discovered"
102	        # from zero data is not data-surfaced at all; it would confirm cleanly on the held-out
103	        # set and silently launder a hand-authored claim through Firewall B. Fail loud.
104	        raise FirewallViolation(
105	            "discovery partition selects no corpus units — nothing to discover from "
106	            "(do split ids match the corpus unit ids?)"
107	        )
108	    hypotheses = list(discover(discovery_corpus))  # sees only the discovery partition
109	    if not hypotheses:
110	        # discovery surfaced nothing — a discovery run with zero hypotheses produces no
111	        # confirmatory verdict at all and is indistinguishable from a silently broken
112	        # ``discover`` callable that forgot to return. Fail loud rather than complete vacuously
113	        # (pass 3, #F-042; mirrors the empty-partition guards above).
114	        raise FirewallViolation(
115	            "discover() returned no hypotheses — a discovery run with zero hypotheses is "
116	            "vacuous and cannot produce a confirmatory verdict"
117	        )
118	
119	    held_out = subset_corpus(corpus, split.confirm_ids)  # disjoint from discovery (split invariant)
120	    if not held_out.units:
121	        # the confirmation partition selects no actual corpus units — confirming on an empty
122	        # held-out set is a vacuous test (the split ids don't match the corpus). Fail loud.
123	        raise FirewallViolation(
124	            "confirmation partition selects no corpus units — nothing to confirm on "
125	            "(do split ids match the corpus unit ids?)"
126	        )
127	    verdicts: list[Verdict] = []
128	    # within-run hypothesis uniqueness (pass 3, #F-018): a ``discover`` step returning the same
129	    # hypothesis_id twice would append two verdicts for one hypothesis, inflating the verdict
130	    # count. The pipeline runner guards this via ``_require_unique_ids``; mirror it in the
131	    # public standalone runner so both entry points enforce the same identity invariant.
132	    seen_hypothesis_ids: set[str] = set()
133	    for hypothesis in hypotheses:
134	        if hypothesis.hypothesis_id in seen_hypothesis_ids:
135	            raise FirewallViolation(
136	                f"duplicate hypothesis_id {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} returned by discover() — "
137	                "each discovered hypothesis must have a unique id"
138	            )
139	        seen_hypothesis_ids.add(hypothesis.hypothesis_id)
140	        if hypothesis.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY:
141	            raise FirewallViolation(
142	                f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is {hypothesis.origin.value!r}; the "
143	                "discovery runner expects DISCOVERY-origin (data-surfaced) hypotheses"
144	            )
145	        # Pre-registration enforced by the runner: the proof must bind THIS hypothesis's content
146	        # (no post-lock content swap) and its attested lock time must precede this confirmation.
147	        require_preregistered(
148	            hypothesis, authority=authority, not_after=_dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc)
149	        )
150	        verdict = confirm(hypothesis, held_out)
151	        if verdict.hypothesis_id != hypothesis.hypothesis_id:
152	            raise FirewallViolation(
153	                f"verdict reports hypothesis_id {verdict.hypothesis_id!r} for hypothesis "
154	                f"{hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
155	            )
156	        verdicts.append(verdict)
157	    return verdicts

content.text: The finding is confirmed at the source. Line 150-151 in discovery.py goes straight from `verdict = confirm(...)` to `verdict.hypothesis_id` with no `isinstance(verdict, Verdict)` guard, while adjudication.py:253 has exactly that guard. Let me verify the pipeline.py:563 location too and confirm no guard exists elsewhere.

content.text:
No test pins a None-verdict guard for discovery (only duplicate/empty/origin guards). There's no `confirm returned` or `NoneType` test for the discovery path. The asymmetry is real and untested.

Let me do a final empirical check: actually run the PoC to confirm the AttributeError is raised rather than a FirewallViolation.

input.description: Read a passing discovery test to mirror fixture setup

content.text: I have a complete fixture template. Let me run the PoC empirically using the test's own helpers.

The PoC reproduces exactly as described. A confirmer returning None raises `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'hypothesis_id'` instead of a typed `FirewallViolation`, while the sibling entry points (adjudication.py:253, pipeline.py:563) both guard this with `if not isinstance(verdict, Verdict): raise FirewallViolation(...)`.

I genuinely could not disprove the finding. The asymmetry is real, untested, and empirically confirmed.

input.reasoning:
Confirmed by reading source and by empirical PoC. In /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/methodology/discovery.py the discover_and_confirm runner calls `verdict = confirm(hypothesis, held_out)` (line 150) and immediately accesses `verdict.hypothesis_id` (line 151) with NO type guard. The two sibling Firewall-B entry points DO guard it: adjudication.py:253 (`if not isinstance(verdict, Verdict): raise FirewallViolation(...)`, pass-3 #F-021) and src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:563 (same guard). grep for `isinstance(verdict, Verdict)` in discovery.py returns nothing, confirming the omission.

The module docstring (discovery.py:94-97) promises FirewallViolation on the enumerated misclassifications including verdict<->hypothesis misattribution, but a None verdict never reaches the misattribution check at line 151 — it dereferences first.

Empirical disproof attempt: ran the PoC using the repo's own test fixtures (tests.test_discovery._corpus/_split/_locked_discovery/_AUTH) with a `bad_confirm` that returns None. Result: `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'hypothesis_id'` — NOT a FirewallViolation. No earlier guard (empty-partition, duplicate-id, origin, require_preregistered) intercepts a None verdict; they all run before confirm() is called.

No test pins the None-verdict-->FirewallViolation behavior for the discovery path (tests/test_discovery.py only covers duplicate/empty/origin/preregistration guards), so nothing makes the concern moot. The typed-exception contract is broken for this one public entry point. Finding survives.

content.text:
I have completed the adversarial refutation attempt. The finding R-1 survives.

The asymmetry is real and empirically confirmed: `discover_and_confirm` in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/methodology/discovery.py` (lines 150-151) dereferences `verdict.hypothesis_id` with no `isinstance(verdict, Verdict)` guard, whereas both sibling entry points — `adjudication.py:253` and `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:563` — explicitly guard it and raise `FirewallViolation`. Running the PoC against the repo's own test fixtures produced `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'hypothesis_id'` instead of the documented typed exception, and no earlier guard or test makes it moot.
